steps.title: Identify the slope of line l using perpendicularity
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes multiply to $-1$. Since line $k$ has slope $m$, the slope of line $l$ is:
$$m_{\text{line } l} \cdot m = -1$$$$m_{\text{line } l} = -\frac{1}{m}$$

steps.title: Use point-slope form to write the equation of line l
Substitute $x = 3$ to find the $y$-coordinate
steps.content:
Plug $x = 3$ into the equation of line $l$:
$$y - 9 = -\frac{1}{m}(3 - 2)$$$$y - 9 = -\frac{1}{m}$$$$y = 9 - \frac{1}{m}$$
The point that lies on line $l$ is $\left(3, 9 - \frac{1}{m}\right)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
